title: Phenomenon 1-A
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE: Phenomenon 1-A: Linear No-Clipping}}
FILE ATTACHMENT ACCESSING ARCHIVED CONCERN REPORT...
FILE ATTACHED TO PRIMARY ARTICLE.
CLEARANCE ACCEPTABLE
RECOMMENDATION: PRELIMINARY READING OF ARTICLE.
{{Phenomenon Class|frequency=5|effectiveness=5|danger=2}} == Overview: == {{Blurimage|file=Chair.jpeg|align=right|caption=A chair no-clipping linearly after being dropped from a height.}}Unlike traditional [[The Void|No-Clipping]], Linear No-Clipping utilizes the speed from the subject that is No-Clipping to bypass the traditional methods of entering and exiting a level and instead directly head to the level numerically after it. However, the speed needed to achieve even the minimal levels of Linear No-Clipping are incredibly high for the average person to achieve when carrying items normally used by Wanderers. The weight of a wanderer, though, either part of the subject or something they are carrying, is irrelevant to the total speed needed to trigger linear no-clipping. It is currently unknown if the Linear aspect of Linear No-Clipping is attached to the [[Phenomenon 13|Casper-Bray Numbering System]] or has adapted to Wanderers creating numbering systems. == Levels == There are multiple levels of Linear No-Clipping, all of which can be accessed by wanderers, though at varying speeds. * '''Level 1''' Linear No-Clipping is the easiest level of Linear No-Clipping to achieve. It only requires a speed of 10 miles an hour. However, it cannot be achieved on standard surfaces, and must be done on surfaces that are used to no-clip traditionally. Can travel 5 Levels. * '''Level 2''' Linear No-Clipping is the second level and requires a speed of 13 MPH to achieve. It can be done on no-clippable surfaces as well as "weak surfaces" which are in the radius of a no-clippable surface. Can travel 6.5 Levels. * '''Level 3''' is much harder than Level 2, and requires a speed of 15 MPH to achieve, and can be done on most standard surfaces that are already structurally weak in the real world. Can travel 7.5 Levels. * '''Level 4''' is the hardest level in Linear No-Clipping and requires a speed of 20 MPH to achieve. It can be done on any surface, and provides the furthest reach than any other level. Can travel 10 Levels. == Technique == While the running portion of Linear No-Clipping can be exhausting, what is much more important is both landing at your destination and choosing your destination, both of which involve very specific steps. === Choosing a Destination === Once you exit out of a level and into The Void, you can calculate your max reach very easily. Every MPH that was put into your Level results in you having enough energy to move "half" a level. For example, if you were going 10 miles an hour, you can travel 5 levels maximum. If you're in Level 0, you can get to Level 5 without needing to Linear No-Clip again. It can also be used to access sublevels, though that is more oriented towards landing rather than choosing a destination. To travel backwards (like from Level 10 to 5), as soon as you enter the Void, you must orient yourself backwards to the direction you entered. This is most easily done by simply running forward into the no-clippable surface, jumping, turning around, then entering the void, which sends you back without having to re-orient during the travel time. A general rule of thumb is that every 5 seconds within the void after a successful Linear No-Clip is a single Level travelled. If you want to access a sublevel, you would begin your landing before the next 5 seconds pass. === Landing at your Destination === Landing is the simplest part of the entire Linear No-Clipping process. In order to stop, you must raise your Void Drag Coefficient, which determines your drag in the void. In simpler words, you must spread your arms and legs into an X and you will manifest into your destination. However, if one fails to raise the Void Drag Coefficient, they will travel the maximum distance possible with their Level. With some Levels like 1 or 4, you would go to a main Level, unless you clipped out from a sublevel. If you clip from a sublevel and end up at a Level without a sublevel, you might get "slingshotted" to a nearby Level that wasn't your destination. For example, say you are in a Level 0 sublevel, and use a Level 1 Linear No-Clip to clip to Level 5. While there isn't a number that specifically matches any sublevel number, you will generally go to the highest sublevel there is. This is different if you clip from a Level like Level 11 to Level 15. Level 15 does not have any sublevels, so instead, you may either go to the Level that you intended, or you might get thrown several Levels ahead. == Limitations == While Linear No-Clipping has become known as an always available and easily accessible "get out of danger card", there are certain places where Linear No-Clipping is either severely limited or not available at all. These are; * The Void itself. * Most anomalous Levels with the exceptions of [[The End]], and [[The Hub]] * [[The Frontrooms]] and [[The Broken]] == History == {{Blurquote|image=MyspecificDarkness.jpg|quote-text="And then he was just gone! Gone man! Gone!"|person=Andrew. Last Name Unknown.}}The existence of Linear No-Clipping has debatably always existed and allows entry into the Backrooms, however most scientific officials have instead filed entry into the Backrooms under standard No-Clipping due to speed not being a factor and the destination being the same no matter the method of entry (most of the time.). When it comes to the existence of the term Linear No-Clipping, it becomes much more recent.{{Blurimage|file=NoclipHall.webp|caption=Hallway where Michael No-clipped linearly.|align=right}}During a small venture from their community in [[Level 11.2]], child residents Michael (Last name unknown) and Andrew (Last name unknown) were skateboarding through an area of 11.2 that is much weaker than the rest of the level. Michael proceeded to mess up on an Ollie, fall down with incredible speed (specifically 10 MPH which resulted in a Level 2 Linear No-Clip), and phase through the floor. Andrew quickly rushed back to their outpost to report the event, who then alerted a nearby M.E.G. outposts. During this time, Michael manifested into [[Level 15]]. However, due to the mass availability of access into [[The Database]], he quickly found his way back to Level 11 by breaking several windows. Once back, he made his way to an M.E.G. outpost, where he was questioned about his experience and put into a quarantine for research.{{Blurimage|file=LATROOMBA.webp|caption=LAT after a successful launch. Before Pull-Along technology was developed.|align=right}}From there, M.E.G. started to experiment with Linear No-Clipping, which was called Void-Pierce Clipping at the time. This resulted in the invention of the Linear Acceleration Transport or LAT, a small, modified Roomba designed to accelerate to Level 2 Linear No-Clip speeds before launching the rider through a weak surface. Since then, LATs have become a central part of any advanced and group backed exploration due to the ability to ease the method of traveling and reduce the level of danger. Alongside LATs, stationary LATS or S-LATs were invented, which are significantly more powerful at the cost of mobility. The furthest an S-LAT has transported somebody is from Level 10 to 20. This ability to skip 10 whole levels has led to an initiative to build an S-LAT device at 10 Level intervals to ensure quick and safe travel between Levels. The invention of LATs has also made the discovery of new Levels incredibly easy, even for freshman wanderer, and the rate of new Levels entering the Database has been steadily increasing.

Void-Pierce Concerns
[[File:MEGBUTINVERTED.png]]
''Stellae ubi nullae sunt''

'''Department: LAT/S-LAT Development Department'''
'''Employee ID:''' LAT-H-001
'''Type of Concern:''' Possible Anomaly
'''Further Description:'''

Recently, we've been testing out a theory of an advanced 5th Level of Linear No-Clipping, and have been pushing our S-LAT on Level 10 to its limits. During this time, however, the small hole generated by an object piercing into the Void has both been getting larger and staying longer. This has led to some concern among our scientific staff about it staying permanently once we punch through with enough force. While we have no scientific evidence to actually back that up, it still remains a possibility. While we are shuttering our Level 5 Discovery program, we personally suspect that other S-LAT stations could be experimenting with the same idea. I am recommending that a notice be put out to all stations ordering that any program to discover a 5th Level be shut down until further experimentation proves it safe.

'''Personal Comments:'''

I'm going to be honest, this shit creeps me out. Waking up every day for the past week only to be greeting by the lingering presence into a pitch black void would be bad enough, but occasionally, at night, I'll wake up and see small white specks floating out there. I don't like it. We can already travel 10 levels at a time. That's enough. Also, may not be related, I've been losing track of stuff. My mug went missing, some magnets fell off of my fridge, and a bunch of other stuff has been reported missing around the station. At some point, I'm going to wake up and the entire station is going to vanish in front of my eyes.

'''Board Vote:''' Continue experimentation with Level 5 Linear No-Clipping in all stations?
'''Result:''' No. Elimination of experimentation underway.

'''Board Vote:''' Continue experimentation with Level 5 Linear No-Clipping at Origin Site?
'''Result:''' Yes.
